title: Comforting Crunchy Mexican Rice Enchiladas
description: A lazy Sunday Mexican dish featuring herbes-de-provence and fry-grind-rice-khao-khua, this recipe is egg-free friendly and perfect for a comforting meal. The combination of crunchy texture and herbal flavor will leave you wanting more.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the herbes-de-provence and cook for an additional minute.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked rice, fry-grind-rice-khao-khua, and cooked onion mixture.
Spoon about 1/2 cup of the rice mixture onto the center of a tortilla and roll up.
Place the rolled tortillas seam-side down in a baking dish and repeat with the remaining tortillas and rice mixture.
Pour the salsa over the top of the rolled tortillas and sprinkle with shredded cheese.
Cover the dish with aluminum foil and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove the foil and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
